docs.title: Tusmo Documentation
docs.text: Welcome to Tusmo, a Somali programming language!

docs.title: What is Tusmo?
docs.text: Tusmo is a statically-typed programming language that compiles to C, then to a binary using Zig. It uses Somali keywords and syntax, making it accessible to Somali developers.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Install Tusmo
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Tusmolang/Tusmo/main/install.sh | bash

# Run a Tusmo program
tusmo hello.tus

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
hello.tus  →  tusmo (parser)  →  hello.c  →  zig cc  →  hello (binary)  →  ./hello

Tusmo compiles .tus files to C code, then uses Zig (bundled) to compile to an executable.

The bundled release includes everything needed - no external dependencies required.

docs.title: Language Features
docs.text:
• Static Typing - Variables have explicit types
  • Somali Keywords - Native Somali syntax (keyd, haddii, soco, etc.)
  • Object-Oriented - Classes and inheritance
  • Garbage Collection - Uses Boehm GC (bundled)
  • Built-in Standard Library - File I/O, HTTP, sockets, time, random

docs.title: Documentation Sections
docs.text:
Getting Started: - Getting Started - Installation and first program - How It Works - Internal architecture and compiler pipeline

Language Reference: - Variables - Data types and declarations - Strings - String operations - Arrays - Array and 2D arrays - Dictionaries - Key-value pairs - Conditions - If/else statements - Loops - While, for, foreach - Functions - Function definitions - Classes - Object-oriented programming - Operators - Arithmetic and comparisons

Standard Library: - Standard Library - Overview - OS Module - File system and system operations - Wakhti Module - Time and date functions - Nasiib Module - Random number generation - Xiriiriye Module - TCP socket programming - WebXiriiriye Module - WebSocket support - HTTP Module - HTTP server and requests

docs.title: Arrays
docs.text: Arrays in Tusmo use tix:<type> syntax.

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text:
keyd:tix:tiro nums = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
keyd:tix:eray words = ["foo", "bar", "baz"];
keyd:tix:jajab floats = [1.1, 2.2, 3.3];
keyd:tix:miyaa flags = [haa, maya, haa];
keyd:tix:tiro empty = [];

docs.title: Access Elements
docs.text:
qor(nums[0]);  // First element
qor(nums[2]);  // Third element

docs.title: Modify Elements
docs.text:
keyd:tix:tiro arr = [10, 20, 30];
arr[0] = 100;

docs.title: Array Length
docs.text: qor(dherer(nums));

docs.title: Iterate with For-Each
docs.text:
soco item kasta laga helo nums {
    qor(item);
}

docs.title: 2D Arrays
docs.text:
keyd:tix:tix:tiro matrix = [[1, 2], [3, 4], [5, 6]];

qor(matrix[0][0]);  // 1
qor(matrix[1][1]);  // 4

keyd:tix:tix:eray stringMatrix = [["a", "b"], ["c", "d"]];
qor(stringMatrix[0][0]);  // "a"

docs.title: Arrays with Expressions
docs.text:
keyd:tiro val1 = 1;
keyd:tiro val2 = 2;
keyd:tix:tiro exprArr = [val1 + val2, val1 * val2, val2 - val1];

docs.title: Classes
docs.text: Tusmo supports object-oriented programming with classes using the koox keyword.

docs.title: Class Definition
docs.text:
koox Qof {
    keyd:eray name;
    keyd:tiro age;

    dhis(n: eray) : waxbo {
        kan.name = n;
        kan.age = 0;
    }

    hawl soo_dhah() : waxbo {
        qor("Name: " + kan.name);
    }

    hawl setAge(a: tiro) : waxbo {
        kan.age = a;
    }

    hawl getAge() : tiro {
        soo_celi kan.age;
    }
}

docs.title: Create Instance
docs.text:
Use cusub keyword to create a new instance:

keyd:Qof p1 = Qof("Ali") cusub;
p1.setAge(25);
p1.soo_dhah();
qor(p1.getAge());

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
The dhis method acts as the constructor:

dhis(n: eray) : waxbo {
    kan.name = n;
    kan.age = 0;
}

docs.title: Self Reference
docs.text:
Use kan to refer to the current instance:

kan.name = n;
kan.age = a;
soo_celi kan.age;

docs.title: Inheritance
docs.text:
Use dhaxlaya keyword for inheritance:

koox Baabuur {
    keyd:tiro taayiro;
    keyd:eray nooca;

    dhis(t: tiro) : waxbo {
        kan.taayiro = t;
        kan.nooca = "Baabuur";
    }

    hawl socoshada() : waxbo {
        qor("Baabuur wuu socdaa");
    }
}

koox Toyota dhaxlaya Baabuur {
    keyd:eray model;

    dhis(m: eray) : waxbo {
        waalid.dhis(4);
        kan.model = m;
    }

    hawl socoshada() : waxbo {
        waalid.socoshada();
        qor("Toyota " + kan.model);
    }
}

Use waalid to reference the parent class:

waalid.socoshada();  // Call parent method

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
koox Player {
    keyd:eray name;
    keyd:miyaa alive;
    keyd:tiro score;

    dhis(n: eray) : waxbo {
        kan.name = n;
        kan.alive = haa;
        kan.score = 0;
    }

    hawl hit() : waxbo {
        kan.alive = maya;
    }

    hawl addScore(points: tiro) : waxbo {
        kan.score = kan.score + points;
    }

    hawl isAlive() : miyaa {
        soo_celi kan.alive;
    }

    hawl getScore() : tiro {
        soo_celi kan.score;
    }
}

keyd:Player player = Player("Hero") cusub;
qor(player.name);
qor(" is alive: ");
qor(player.isAlive());
player.hit();
player.addScore(100);
qor("Score: ");
qor(player.getScore());

docs.title: Class Keywords
docs.text: Keyword Description koox Class declaration dhaxlaya Inheritance keyword waalid Parent class reference cusub Create new instance kan Self reference dhis Constructor hawl Method declaration

docs.title: Conditions
docs.text: Tusmo uses Somali keywords for conditional statements.

docs.title: If Statement
docs.text:
haddii (x > 5) {
    qor("x is greater than 5");
}

docs.title: If-Else
docs.text:
haddii (x < 5) {
    qor("x is less than 5");
} haddii_kale {
    qor("x is NOT less than 5");
}

docs.title: If-Elif-Else
docs.text:
haddii (x == 5) {
    qor("x equals 5");
} ama_haddii (x == 10) {
    qor("x equals 10");
} ama_haddii (x == 15) {
    qor("x equals 15");
} haddii_kale {
    qor("x is something else");
}

docs.title: Nested Conditions
docs.text:
haddii (x > 5) {
    qor("x > 5");
    haddii (y > 15) {
        qor("y > 15 too");
    }
}

docs.title: Comparison Operators
docs.text: Operator Description == Equal != Not equal < Less than > Greater than <= Less than or equal >= Greater than or equal

docs.title: Boolean Operators
Ternary Operator
docs.text: keyd:miyaa big = x > y ? haa : maya;

docs.title: Dictionaries
docs.text: Dictionaries in Tusmo use qaamuus type for key-value pairs.

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text:
keyd:qaamuus emptyDict;
keyd:qaamuus person = {"magac": "Ali", "da": 25};

docs.title: Access Values
docs.text:
qor(person["magac"]);  // "Ali"
qor(person["da"]);     // 25

docs.title: Modify Values
docs.text: person["da"] = 26;

docs.title: Add New Keys
docs.text: person["phone"] = "612345678";

docs.title: Nested Dictionaries
docs.text:
keyd:qaamuus nested = {
    "user": {
        "username": "admin",
        "email": "admin@test.com"
    }
};

qor(nested["user"]["username"]);

docs.title: Different Value Types
docs.text:
keyd:qaamuus mixed = {
    "name": "Test",
    "age": 20,
    "score": 95.5,
    "active": haa
};

docs.title: Arrays in Dictionaries
docs.text:
keyd:qaamuus scores = {
    "math": [90, 85, 88],
    "science": [95, 92]
};

qor(scores["math"]);

docs.title: Dictionary of Dictionaries
docs.text:
keyd:qaamuus students = {
    "ali": {"grade": "A", "score": 95},
    "sara": {"grade": "B", "score": 88}
};

docs.title: Functions
docs.text: Tusmo supports functions with two syntax styles.

docs.title: Function Syntax
docs.text: Tusmo supports two function syntax styles:

docs.title: Colon Style (void return)
docs.text:
hawl greet(name: eray) : waxbo {
    qor("Hello " + name);
}

docs.title: Arrow Style (with return)
docs.text:
shaqo add(a: tiro, b: tiro) => tiro {
    soo_celi a + b;
}

docs.title: Return Values
docs.text:
Use soo_celi to return:

hawl divide(a: jajab, b: jajab) => jajab {
    soo_celi a / b;
}

hawl getMessage() : eray {
    soo_celi "This is a message";
}

docs.title: Parameters with Default Values
docs.text:
hawl greetDefault(name: eray, prefix: eray = "Mr.") : waxbo {
    qor("Hello " + prefix + " " + name);
}

docs.title: No Parameters
docs.text:
hawl sayHello() : waxbo {
    qor("Just saying Hello!");
}

docs.title: Return Boolean
docs.text:
hawl isEven(n: tiro) : miyaa {
    keyd:miyaa res = (n % 2) == 0;
    soo_celi res;
}

docs.title: Array Parameters
docs.text:
hawl sumArray(arr: tix:tiro) : tiro {
    keyd:tiro total = 0;
    soco item kasta laga helo arr {
        total = total + item;
    }
    soo_celi total;
}

docs.title: Function Keywords
docs.text: Keyword Description hawl Function declaration (colon style) shaqo Function declaration (arrow style) soo_celi Return value : waxbo Void return type => <type> Return type (arrow style)

docs.title: Calling Functions
docs.text:
greet("Tusmo");
keyd:tiro result = add(5, 3);
qor(divide(10.0, 4.0));

docs.title: Linux/macOS
docs.text: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Tusmolang/Tusmo/main/install.sh | bash

docs.title: Windows
docs.text: irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Tusmolang/Tusmo/main/install.ps1 | iex

docs.title: Your First Program
docs.text:
Create hello.tus:

qor("Hello, Somaliland!");

docs.title: Running Tusmo
docs.text:
Simply pass the .tus file to the tusmo command:

tusmo hello.tus

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
tusmo <file.tus> [options]

Options:
  --c           Keep the generated C code file
  -h, --help    Show help
  -v, --version Show version
  -l, --libraries List supported libraries
  update        Update Tusmo to latest version
  install <lib> Install a library

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
hello.tus  →  tusmo (parser)  →  hello.c  →  zig cc  →  hello (binary)  →  ./hello
              ↓
         AST → semantic analysis → C code generation
              ↓
         runtime/*.c + stdlib/*.tus → linked

docs.title: Compilation Pipeline
docs.text:
1. Lexing - Tokenize Tusmo source code
  2. Parsing - Build AST (Abstract Syntax Tree)
  3. Semantic Analysis - Type checking, symbol resolution
  4. Code Generation - Generate C code from AST
  5. Compilation - Zig compiles C to binary executable
  6. Execution - Run the binary

The bundled release includes: - Zig compiler - Compiles C to binary - Boehm GC - Garbage collector (prebuilt) - Runtime library - String, array, dictionary support - Standard library - OS, HTTP, sockets, etc.

You do NOT need to install GCC or libgc manually.

docs.title: Keep C Code
docs.text:
Use --c flag to keep the generated C file:

tusmo hello.tus --c

This produces hello.c in the same directory.

docs.title: Code Example
docs.text:
// My first Tusmo program
keyd:eray magac = "Tusmo";
keyd:tiro da = 5;

qor($"Hello, {magac}!");
qor($"Age: {da}");

Output:

Hello, Tusmo!
Age: 5

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
None! The bundled release includes everything needed: - Zig compiler - Boehm GC - Runtime libraries

Just download and run.

docs.title: How Tusmo Works
docs.text: This document explains the internal workings of the Tusmo compiler.

docs.title: Architecture Overview
docs.text:
source.tus  →  Lexer  →  Parser  →  AST  →  Semantic  →  Transpiler  →  C Code
                                     ↓                                         ↓
                               Symbol Table                              zig cc
                                                                              ↓
                                                                        binary

docs.title: 1. Lexing (Tokenization)
docs.text:
The lexer converts Tusmo source code into tokens:

# compiler/frontend/lexer/lexer.py
keywords = {
    'haddii': 'HADDII',
    'keen': 'KEEN',
    'tiro': 'TIRO',
    'eray': 'ERAY',
    '___c__call_': 'C_CALL',
    # ... more keywords
}

docs.title: 2. Parsing (AST Generation)
docs.text:
The parser builds an Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) from tokens:

Program
  └── VariableDeclaration (keyd:tiro x = 5)
        ├── name: "x"
        ├── type: "tiro"
        └── value: 5 (IntegerLiteral)

docs.title: 3. Semantic Analysis
docs.text:
Type checking and symbol resolution:

  • Variable names are registered in the symbol table
  • Type checking ensures type safety
  • Function calls are validated

docs.title: 4. Code Generation (Transpilation)
docs.text:
The transpiler converts AST to C code:

// Generated C code from keyd:tiro x = 5;
int x;
x = 5;

docs.title: The keen Keyword (Import)
docs.text:
The keen keyword imports modules. The compiler resolves the path:

keen "os";

Resolution order: 1. Current directory 2. .lib/ directory 3. lib/ directory 4. stdlib/ directory (built-in)

The imported module's AST is merged with the main program's AST.

docs.title: The ___c__call_ Function
docs.text:
This is a special builtin that calls C functions directly from Tusmo code:

// In stdlib/os.tus
hawl halkee() : eray {
    soo_celi ___c__call_("tusmo_os_cwd");
}

How it works: 1. Parser creates a CCallNode with the C function name 2. Transpiler generates: tusmo_os_cwd() 3. The C function must exist in the runtime

Why use it? - Call C runtime functions (file I/O, sockets, time) - Access system-level functionality - Performance-critical operations

docs.title: The ___c__code_ Function
docs.text:
Embed raw C code directly:

___c_code_("#include <math.h>");

This copies the C code directly into the generated output.

docs.title: Runtime Linking
docs.text:
Tusmo automatically detects which runtime features are needed:

# compiler/backend/transpiler/expression_generator.py

def _generate_ccall(self, node):
    c_function_name = node.c_function_name

    if "tusmo_os" in c_function_name:
        self.main_generator.used_features.add("os")
    elif "tusmo_time" in c_function_name:
        self.main_generator.used_features.add("wakhti")
    elif "tusmo_socket" in c_function_name:
        self.main_generator.used_features.add("socket")

The runtime includes: - runtime/string.c - String operations - runtime/array.c - Array operations - runtime/dictionary.c - Dictionary operations - runtime/os.c - OS functions - runtime/time.c - Time functions - runtime/socket.c - Socket functions - runtime/http.c - HTTP server

docs.title: Generated C Code Structure
docs.text:
#include "tusmo_runtime.h"

int main(void) {
    GC_INIT();

    // Your code here

    return 0;
}

The runtime header provides: - GC_INIT() - Initialize garbage collector - TusmoString - String type - TusmoTixTiro - Integer array type - TusmoQaamuus - Dictionary type - Helper functions

docs.title: Class Implementation
docs.text:
Classes are implemented as C structs with function pointers:

// Generated for koox Qof { keyd:eray name; }
typedef struct Qof {
    char* name;
    // ... more fields
} Qof;

// Method
void Qof_setName(Qof* kan, char* name) {
    kan->name = name;
}

docs.title: Memory Management
docs.text:
Tusmo uses the Boehm garbage collector:

#include <gc.h>

int main(void) {
    GC_INIT();
    // All allocations are automatic
}

No manual malloc/free needed!

docs.title: Custom C Functions
docs.text:
You can add your own C functions to the runtime:

  1. Add function to a .c file in runtime/
  2. Declare in runtime/tusmo_runtime.h
  3. Call from Tusmo with ___c__call_("your_function")

Example:

// runtime/myext.c
int tusmo_my_add(int a, int b) {
    return a + b;
}

// runtime/tusmo_runtime.h
extern int tusmo_my_add(int a, int b);

// In your .tus file
keyd:tiro result = ___c__call_("tusmo_my_add", 3, 4);

docs.title: Build Process
docs.text:
When you run tusmo hello.tus:

  1. Parse hello.tus + all imports
  2. Build AST
  3. Type check
  4. Generate hello.c
  5. Compile: zig cc hello.c runtime/*.c -lgc -o hello
  6. Execute ./hello

The bundled release includes everything needed - no external dependencies.

docs.title: Loops
docs.text: Tusmo supports multiple loop types.

docs.title: While Loop
docs.text:
keyd:tiro i = 0;
inta ay (i < 5) {
    qor("While: " + i);
    i = i + 1;
}

docs.title: Do-While Loop
docs.text:
keyd:tiro j = 0;
samay {
    qor("Do-While: " + j);
    j = j + 1;
} inta ay (j < 5);

docs.title: For Range Loop
docs.text:
soco k laga bilaabo 0 .. 5 {
    qor("For Range: " + k);
}

docs.title: For-Each Loop
docs.text:
keyd:tix:eray arr = ["aa", "bb", "cc"];
soco item kasta laga helo arr {
    qor("For Each: " + item);
}

docs.title: Break
docs.text:
keyd:tiro n = 0;
inta ay (n < 10) {
    n = n + 1;
    haddii (n == 5) {
        joog;
    }
    qor("Break test: " + n);
}

docs.title: Continue
docs.text:
soco m laga bilaabo 0 .. 5 {
    haddii (m == 2) {
        kasoco;
    }
    qor("Continue test: " + m);
}

docs.title: Nested Loops
docs.text:
keyd:tiro outer = 0;
inta ay (outer < 3) {
    keyd:tiro inner = 0;
    soco inner_loop laga bilaabo 0 .. 3 {
        haddii (outer == 1 iyo inner == 1) {
            joog;
        }
        qor($"Outer: {outer}, Inner: {inner}");
        inner = inner + 1;
    }
    outer = outer + 1;
}

docs.title: Loop Keywords Summary
docs.text: Keyword Description inta ay ... While condition samay ... Do-while soco ... For/foreach joog Break kasoco Continue laga bilaabo Range start .. Range kasta laga helo For each iteration

docs.title: Operators
docs.text: Tusmo supports various operators for arithmetic, comparison, and boolean operations.

docs.title: Arithmetic Operators
docs.text:
keyd:tiro a = 10;
keyd:tiro b = 3;

qor(a + b);   // Addition: 13
qor(a - b);   // Subtraction: 7
qor(a * b);   // Multiplication: 30
qor(a / b);   // Division: 3
qor(a % b);   // Modulus: 1

docs.title: Increment/Decrement
docs.text:
keyd:tiro x = 0;
x = x + 1;    // Increment
x = x - 1;    // Decrement

docs.title: Assignment Operators
docs.text:
keyd:tiro y = 0;
y = y + 3;
y = y - 2;
y = y * 2;

docs.title: Comparison Operators
docs.text:
keyd:tiro p = 5;
keyd:tiro q = 10;

qor(p == q);  // Equal: maya
qor(p != q);  // Not equal: haa
qor(p < q);   // Less than: haa
qor(p > q);   // Greater than: maya
qor(p <= q);  // Less or equal: haa
qor(p >= q);  // Greater or equal: maya

Float comparison:

keyd:jajab f1 = 3.14;
keyd:jajab f2 = 2.5;
qor(f1 > f2);  // haa

docs.title: Boolean Operators
Ternary Operator
docs.text:
keyd:tiro num = 5;
keyd:miyaa isBig = num > 10;
keyd:eray result = isBig ? "big" : "small";

docs.title: Operator Precedence
docs.text:
keyd:tiro prec = 2 + 3 * 4;    // 14 (multiplication first)
prec = (2 + 3) * 4;            // 20 (parentheses first)

docs.title: HTTP Module
docs.text: The HTTP module provides HTTP server functionality.

docs.title: Import
docs.text: keen "http";

docs.title: Http Class
docs.text:
Create an HTTP server.

keyd:Http server = Http() cusub;

docs.title: dhegeyso(port, handler)
docs.text:
Start HTTP server on a port.

hawl maamule(codsi: Codsi) : waxbo {
    qor("Request: " + codsi.waddo());
    codsi.jawaab_caadi("<h1>Hello!</h1>");
}

server.dhegeyso("8080", maamule);

Parameters: - port (eray) - Port number (e.g., "8080") - maamule (hawl) - Handler function that takes a Codsi object

docs.title: jooji()
docs.text:
Stop the server.

server.jooji();

docs.title: Codsi Class
docs.text: The Codsi class represents an HTTP request. It's passed to your handler function.

docs.title: hab()
docs.text:
Get HTTP method.

keyd:eray method = codsi.hab();
// Returns: "HELID" (GET), "DHIGID" (POST), etc.

Returns: eray - HTTP method

docs.title: waddo()
docs.text:
Get request path/URL.

keyd:eray path = codsi.waddo();
// e.g., "/api/users"

Returns: eray - Request path

docs.title: xambaare()
docs.text:
Get request body.

keyd:eray body = codsi.xambaare();

Returns: eray - Request body content

docs.title: macmiil()
docs.text:
Get client IP address.

keyd:eray client = codsi.macmiil();
// e.g., "192.168.1.100:12345"

Returns: eray - Client address

docs.title: hel_madax(name)
docs.text:
Get HTTP header value.

keyd:eray content_type = codsi.hel_madax("Content-Type");
keyd:eray user_agent = codsi.hel_madax("User-Agent");

Parameters: - magac (eray) - Header name

Returns: eray - Header value

docs.title: jawaab_eray(content, status, content_type)
docs.text:
Send text response.

codsi.jawaab_eray("<h1>Hello</h1>", 200, "text/html; charset=utf-8");

Parameters: - nuxur (eray) - Response content - xaalad (tiro) - HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 404) - nooca (eray) - MIME type (e.g., "text/html")

docs.title: jawaab(data, status, content_type)
docs.text:
Send JSON response.

keyd:qaamuus json_data = {"name": "Tusmo", "version": "1.0"};
codsi.jawaab(json_data, 200, "application/json");

Parameters: - nuxur (qaamuus) - Data to convert to JSON - xaalad (tiro) - HTTP status code - nooca (eray) - MIME type

docs.title: jawaab_caadi(content)
docs.text:
Send HTML response (shortcut).

codsi.jawaab_caadi("<h1>Hello!</h1>");

Parameters: - nuxur (eray) - HTML content

docs.title: hel_socket_handle()
docs.text:
Get underlying socket for WebSocket upgrade.

keyd:eray socket_handle = codsi.hel_socket_handle();

Returns: eray - Socket handle for WebSocket

docs.title: foom()
docs.text:
Parse form data (application/x-www-form-urlencoded).

keyd:Form form = codsi.foom();
keyd:eray name = form.hel("name");
keyd:eray email = form.hel("email");

Returns: Form - Form object with hel(field) method

docs.title: Example: Simple HTTP Server
docs.text:
keen "http";

hawl handle_request(codsi: Codsi) : waxbo {
    keyd:eray path = codsi.waddo();

    haddii (path == "/") {
        codsi.jawaab_caadi("<h1>Welcome to Tusmo!</h1>");
    } ama_haddii (path == "/api/hello") {
        keyd:qaamuus data = {"message": "Hello API"};
        codsi.jawaab(data, 200, "application/json");
    } haddii_kale {
        codsi.jawaab_eray("Not Found", 404, "text/plain");
    }
}

keyd:Http server = Http() cusub;
qor("Server started on port 8080");
server.dhegeyso("8080", handle_request);

docs.title: Example: WebSocket via HTTP
docs.text:
keen "http";
keen "webxiriiriye";

hawl handle_request(codsi: Codsi) : waxbo {
    keyd:eray upgrade = codsi.hel_madax("Upgrade");

    haddii (upgrade == "websocket") {
        keyd:eray socket_handle = codsi.hel_socket_handle();
        keyd:eray ws_key = codsi.hel_madax("Sec-WebSocket-Key");

        keyd:WebXiriiriye ws = WebXiriiriye() cusub;
        ws.kor_u_qaad(socket_handle, ws_key);

        qor("WebSocket connected!");
        ws.xir(1000, "Done");
    } haddii_kale {
        codsi.jawaab_caadi("<h1>WebSocket Server</h1>");
    }
}

keyd:Http server = Http() cusub;
server.dhegeyso("8080", handle_request);

docs.title: Nasiib Module
docs.text: The Nasiib module provides random number generation.

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
keen "nasiib";

Initialize before use (optional - automatic):

nasiib.bilaab_nasiib();

docs.title: bilaab_nasiib()
docs.text:
Initialize the random number generator. Call once before using other functions.

nasiib.bilaab_nasiib();

Returns: waxbo

docs.title: nasiib_tiro(min, max)
docs.text:
Generate random integer between min and max (inclusive).

keyd:tiro num = nasiib.tiro(1, 100);
qor("Random: ");
qor(num);  // 1-100

Parameters: - ugu_yaraan (tiro) - Minimum value - ugu_badnaan (tiro) - Maximum value

Returns: tiro - Random integer

docs.title: nasiib_jajab(min, max)
docs.text:
Generate random float between min and max.

keyd:jajab num = nasiib.jajab(0.0, 1.0);
qor("Random float: ");
qor(num);  // e.g., 0.456789

Parameters: - ugu_yaraan (jajab) - Minimum value - ugu_badnaan (jajab) - Maximum value

Returns: jajab - Random float

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
keen "nasiib";

nasiib.bilaab_nasiib();

// Random integer 1-10
keyd:tiro num1 = nasiib.tiro(1, 10);
qor("Dice roll: ");
qor(num1);

// Random float 0-1
keyd:jajab num2 = nasiib.jajab(0.0, 1.0);
qor("Random float: ");
qor(num2);

docs.title: OS Module
docs.text: The OS module provides file system and system operations.

docs.title: Import
docs.text: keen "os";

docs.title: halkee()
docs.text:
Get the current working directory.

keyd:eray cwd = os.halkee();
qor(cwd);

Returns: eray - Current directory path

docs.title: u_guur(folder)
docs.text:
Change directory.

os.u_guur("/path/to/folder");

Parameters: - folder_kee_aadaa (eray) - Path to change to

docs.title: itus(path)
docs.text:
List directory contents.

keyd:tix:eray files = os.itus("/path/to/folder");
soco item kasta laga helo files {
    qor(item);
}

Parameters: - folder_kee_ku_tusiyaa_waxa_ku_jira (eray) - Directory path

Returns: tix:eray - Array of file/folder names

docs.title: abuur_folder(path)
docs.text:
Create a directory.

os.abuur_folder("myfolder");

Parameters: - path (eray) - Path to create

docs.title: tirtir_folder(path)
docs.text:
Remove a directory.

os.tirtir_folder("myfolder");

Parameters: - path (eray) - Path to remove

docs.title: tirtir_fayl(path)
docs.text:
Delete a file.

os.tirtir_fayl("file.txt");

Parameters: - path (eray) - File path to delete

docs.title: majiraa(path)
docs.text:
Check if path exists.

keyd:miyaa exists = os.majiraa("file.txt");
haddii (exists) {
    qor("File exists!");
}

Parameters: - path (eray) - Path to check

Returns: miyaa - haa if exists, maya otherwise

docs.title: fayl_miyaa(path)
docs.text:
Check if path is a file.

keyd:miyaa isFile = os.fayl_miyaa("file.txt");

Parameters: - path (eray) - Path to check

Returns: miyaa - haa if file, maya otherwise

docs.title: folder_miyaa(path)
docs.text:
Check if path is a directory.

keyd:miyaa isDir = os.folder_miyaa("folder");

Parameters: - path (eray) - Path to check

Returns: miyaa - haa if directory, maya otherwise

docs.title: hel_deegaan(name)
docs.text:
Get environment variable.

keyd:eray path = os.hel_deegaan("PATH");
qor(path);

Parameters: - name (eray) - Variable name

Returns: eray - Variable value

docs.title: deji_deegaan(name, value)
docs.text:
Set environment variable.

os.deji_deegaan("MY_VAR", "hello");

Parameters: - name (eray) - Variable name - value (eray) - Variable value

docs.title: fuli(command)
docs.text:
Execute shell command.

keyd:tiro result = os.fuli("ls -la");

Parameters: - command (eray) - Shell command

Returns: tiro - Exit code

docs.title: koobi(source, destination)
docs.text:
Copy file.

os.koobi("source.txt", "dest.txt");

Parameters: - source (eray) - Source file path - destination (eray) - Destination file path

docs.title: nuqul(source, destination)
docs.text:
Alias for koobi - copy file.

os.nuqul("source.txt", "dest.txt");

docs.title: u_dhaqaaji(old_path, new_path)
docs.text:
Move or rename file/folder.

os.u_dhaqaaji("old.txt", "new.txt");

Parameters: - old_path (eray) - Current path - new_path (eray) - New path

docs.title: aqri_fayl(path)
docs.text:
Read file contents.

keyd:eray content = os.aqri_fayl("file.txt");
qor(content);

Parameters: - path (eray) - File path

Returns: eray - File contents

docs.title: qor_fayl(path, content, append)
docs.text:
Write to file.

// Write (overwrite)
os.qor_fayl("file.txt", "Hello", maya);

// Append
os.qor_fayl("file.txt", " World", haa);

Parameters: - path (eray) - File path - content (eray) - Content to write - append (miyaa) - haa to append, maya to overwrite

docs.title: isku_dar_waddo(part1, part2)
docs.text:
Join path parts.

keyd:eray full = os.isku_dar_waddo("folder", "file.txt");
qor(full);  // "folder/file.txt"

Parameters: - part1 (eray) - First path part - part2 (eray) - Second path part

Returns: eray - Joined path

docs.title: cabbir_fayl(path)
docs.text:
Get file size in bytes.

keyd:tiro size = os.cabbir_fayl("file.txt");
qor("File size: " + nooc(size));

Parameters: - path (eray) - File path

Returns: tiro - File size in bytes

docs.title: Wakhti Module
docs.text: The Wakhti module provides time and date functions.

docs.title: Import
docs.text: keen "wakhti";

docs.title: hadda()
docs.text:
Get current Unix timestamp.

keyd:jajab now = wakhti.hadda();
qor("Timestamp: ");
qor(now);

Returns: jajab - Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch)

docs.title: qaab(format)
docs.text:
Format current time.

keyd:eray formatted = wakhti.qaab("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S");
qor(formatted);  // e.g., "2024-01-15 10:30:45"

Parameters: - format (eray) - Format string

Format specifiers: | Code | Description | |------|-------------| | %Y | Year (4 digits) | | %m | Month (01-12) | | %d | Day (01-31) | | %H | Hour (00-23) | | %M | Minute (00-59) | | %S | Second (00-59) |

Returns: eray - Formatted time string

docs.title: sekeno()
docs.text:
Get current seconds (0-59).

keyd:tiro sec = wakhti.sekeno();
qor("Seconds: ");
qor(sec);

Returns: tiro - Seconds (0-59)

docs.title: daqiiqado()
docs.text:
Get current minutes (0-59).

keyd:tiro min = wakhti.daqiiqado();
qor("Minutes: ");
qor(min);

Returns: tiro - Minutes (0-59)

docs.title: saacado()
docs.text:
Get current hours (0-23).

keyd:tiro hr = wakhti.saacado();
qor("Hour: ");
qor(hr);

Returns: tiro - Hours (0-23)

docs.title: maalin()
docs.text:
Get current day of month (1-31).

keyd:tiro day = wakhti.maalin();
qor("Day: ");
qor(day);

Returns: tiro - Day (1-31)

docs.title: bil()
docs.text:
Get current month (1-12).

keyd:tiro month = wakhti.bil();
qor("Month: ");
qor(month);

Returns: tiro - Month (1-12)

docs.title: sanad()
docs.text:
Get current year.

keyd:tiro year = wakhti.sanad();
qor("Year: ");
qor(year);

Returns: tiro - Full year

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
keen "wakhti";

qor("Current time:");
qor("Hour: " + nooc(wakhti.saacado()));
qor("Minute: " + nooc(wakhti.daqiiqado()));
qor("Second: " + nooc(wakhti.sekeno()));

qor("Date:");
qor("Day: " + nooc(wakhti.maalin()));
qor("Month: " + nooc(wakhti.bil()));
qor("Year: " + nooc(wakhti.sanad()));

docs.title: WebXiriiriye Module
docs.text: The WebXiriiriye module provides WebSocket support for real-time bidirectional communication.

docs.title: Import
docs.text: keen "webxiriiriye";

docs.title: Create WebSocket
docs.text: keyd:WebXiriiriye ws = WebXiriiriye() cusub;

docs.title: kor_u_qaad(socket_handle, ws_key)
docs.text:
Upgrade HTTP connection to WebSocket.

keyd:eray ws_key = "dGhlIHNhbXBsZSBncmF2aXR5";  // From HTTP request header
ws.kor_u_qaad(client_socket_handle, ws_key);

Parameters: - client_socket_handle (eray) - Client socket handle from HTTP server - ws_key (eray) - Sec-WebSocket-Key from HTTP request header

docs.title: dir_qoraal(message)
docs.text:
Send text message.

keyd:tiro sent = ws.dir_qoraal("Hello!");
qor("Sent bytes: ");
qor(sent);

Parameters: - fariin (eray) - Message text

Returns: tiro - Bytes sent, or -1 on error

docs.title: dir_binary(data)
docs.text:
Send binary data.

keyd:tiro sent = ws.dir_binary("\x00\x01\x02");

Parameters: - xog (eray) - Binary data

Returns: tiro - Bytes sent, or -1 on error

docs.title: soo_hel_fariin()
docs.text:
Receive WebSocket message.

keyd:qaamuus msg = ws.soo_hel_fariin();
haddii (msg["xaalad"] == "guul") {
    haddii (msg["nooc"] == "qoraal") {
        qor("Text: ");
        qor(msg["xog"]);
    } ama_haddii (msg["nooc"] == "binary") {
        qor("Binary received");
    } ama_haddii (msg["nooc"] == "xir") {
        qor("Connection closed");
    }
}

Returns: qaamuus - Message object with: - xaalad: "guul" or "qalad" - nooc: "qoraal", "binary", "ping", "pong", or "xir" - xog: Message content

docs.title: dir_ping()
docs.text:
Send ping frame.

keyd:tiro result = ws.dir_ping();

Returns: tiro - 0 on success, -1 on error

docs.title: dir_pong()
docs.text:
Send pong frame.

keyd:tiro result = ws.dir_pong();

Returns: tiro - 0 on success, -1 on error

docs.title: xir(code, reason)
docs.text:
Close WebSocket connection.

ws.xir(1000, "Normal shutdown");

Parameters: - cod (tiro) - Close code (1000 = normal close) - sabab (eray) - Close reason

docs.title: ma_xiran()
docs.text:
Check if WebSocket is connected.

haddii (ws.ma_xiran()) {
    qor("Connected!");
}

Returns: miyaa - haa if connected, maya otherwise

docs.title: Example: WebSocket Server
docs.text:
keen "xiriiriye";
keen "webxiriiriye";
keen "http";

keyd:Xiriiriye server = Xiriiriye() cusub;
keyd:eray handle = server.samee_server("8080");

haddii (handle == "") {
    qor("Server failed!");
    server.xir();
}

server.dhageyso(5);
qor("WebSocket server on port 8080");

keyd:eray client = server.aqbal();
haddii (client == "") {
    qor("No client");
} haddii_kale {
    // Get WebSocket key from HTTP request
    keyd:eray ws_key = http.fariin_weyn(client, "Sec-WebSocket-Key");

    keyd:WebXiriiriye ws = WebXiriiriye() cusub;
    ws.kor_u_qaad(client, ws_key);

    qor("WebSocket connected!");

    // Echo messages
    keyd:qaamuus msg = ws.soo_hel_fariin();
    haddii (msg["xaalad"] == "guul" iyo msg["nooc"] == "qoraal") {
        ws.dir_qoraal(msg["xog"]);  // Echo back
    }

    ws.xir(1000, "Done");
}

server.xir();

docs.title: Xiriiriye Module
docs.text: The Xiriiriye module provides low-level socket programming for TCP connections.

docs.title: Import
docs.text: keen "xiriiriye";

docs.title: Create Socket
docs.text: keyd:Xiriiriye socket = Xiriiriye() cusub;

docs.title: samee_server(port)
docs.text:
Create a server socket listening on a port.

keyd:eray handle = socket.samee_server("8080");
haddii (handle == "") {
    qor("Failed to create server!");
} haddii_kale {
    qor("Server created!");
}

Parameters: - port (eray) - Port number as string (e.g., "8080")

Returns: eray - Socket handle (empty string on failure)

docs.title: dhageyso(max_clients)
docs.text:
Start listening for connections.

keyd:tiro result = socket.dhageyso(5);
haddii (result == 0) {
    qor("Listening...");
}

Parameters: - saf_sugid_ugu_badan (tiro) - Maximum queue length (typically 5-10)

Returns: tiro - 0 on success, -1 on failure

docs.title: aqbal()
docs.text:
Accept incoming client connection.

keyd:eray client_handle = socket.aqbal();
haddii (client_handle == "") {
    qor("No client connected");
} haddii_kale {
    qor("Client connected!");
}

Returns: eray - Client handle (empty string if no connection)

docs.title: kuxirmo(host, port)
docs.text:
Connect to a remote server as client.

keyd:eray handle = socket.kuxirmo("example.com", "80");
haddii (handle == "") {
    qor("Connection failed!");
}

Parameters: - host (eray) - Hostname or IP (e.g., "example.com", "127.0.0.1") - port (eray) - Port as string (e.g., "80")

Returns: eray - Socket handle (empty string on failure)

docs.title: dir(data)
docs.text:
Send data over socket.

keyd:tiro bytes_sent = socket.dir("Hello Server!");
qor("Sent bytes: ");
qor(bytes_sent);

Parameters: - xogta (eray) - Data to send

Returns: tiro - Bytes sent, or -1 on error

docs.title: soo_hel(size)
docs.text:
Receive data from socket.

keyd:eray data = socket.soo_hel(1024);
qor("Received: ");
qor(data);

Parameters: - size (tiro) - Maximum bytes to receive

Returns: eray - Received data

docs.title: xir()
docs.text:
Close the socket.

socket.xir();

docs.title: waa_ansax()
docs.text:
Check if socket is valid.

haddii (socket.waa_ansax()) {
    qor("Socket is open");
}

Returns: miyaa - haa if valid, maya otherwise

docs.title: Server Example
docs.text:
keen "xiriiriye";

keyd:Xiriiriye server = Xiriiriye() cusub;
keyd:eray handle = server.samee_server("9999");

haddii (handle == "") {
    qor("Server creation failed!");
    socket.xir();
}

server.dhageyso(5);
qor("Waiting for client...");

keyd:eray client = server.aqbal();
haddii (client == "") {
    qor("No client connected");
} haddii_kale {
    qor("Client connected!");
    server.dir("Welcome to server!");
}

server.xir();

docs.title: Client Example
docs.text:
keen "xiriiriye";

keyd:Xiriiriye client = Xiriiriye() cusub;
keyd:eray handle = client.kuxirmo("example.com", "80");

haddii (handle == "") {
    qor("Connection failed!");
    client.xir();
}

// Send HTTP request
client.dir("GET / HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: example.com\r\n\r\n");

// Receive response
keyd:eray response = client.soo_hel(4096);
qor(response);

client.xir();

docs.title: Standard Library
docs.text: Tusmo comes with built-in modules in the stdlib/ folder. These are imported using the keen keyword.

docs.title: Built-in Functions
docs.text: These functions are available without importing:

docs.title: Output
docs.text: qor("Hello!");      // Print to console

docs.title: String Functions
docs.text: Function Description Example dherer(str) Get string length dherer("hello") → 5 nooc(value) Convert to string nooc(123) → "123" eray(value) Convert to string eray(3.14) → "3.14" tiro(value) Convert to integer tiro("42") → 42 jajab(value) Convert to float jajab("3.14") → 3.14 miyaa(value) Convert to boolean miyaa("haa") → haa

docs.title: Array Functions
docs.text: Function Description Example tix_cayiman(arr) Get array type Returns element type

docs.title: Importing Modules
docs.text:
keen "os";
keen "wakhti";
keen "nasiib";
keen "xiriiriye";
keen "http";

docs.title: Library Sections
docs.text:
• OS Module - File system and system operations
  • Wakhti Module - Time and date functions
  • Nasiib Module - Random number generation
  • Xiriiriye Module - TCP socket programming
  • WebXiriiriye Module - WebSocket support
  • HTTP Module - HTTP server and request handling

docs.title: Strings
docs.text: Strings in Tusmo use the eray type.

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text:
keyd:eray str1 = "Hello";
keyd:eray str2;
keyd:eray str3 = "";

docs.title: Concatenation
docs.text: keyd:eray str = "Hello" + " " + "World";

docs.title: Length
docs.text: qor(dherer(str1));  // Get string length

docs.title: Indexing
docs.text:
qor(str1[0]);  // First character
qor(str1[1]);  // Second character

docs.title: F-Strings
docs.text:
Use $"{}" for interpolation:

keyd:eray fruit = "Apple";
qor($"I like {fruit}");
qor($"Math: 5 * 3 = {5 * 3}");

Multi-line strings:

keyd:eray multiline = $"This is a
multi-line
string";

docs.title: Comparison
docs.text:
keyd:eray a = "abc";
keyd:eray b = "abc";
keyd:eray c = "def";

qor(a == b);  // haa (yes)
qor(a == c);  // maya (no)

docs.title: Variables
docs.text: Tusmo is a statically-typed language with explicit type declarations.

docs.title: Declaration Syntax
docs.text:
keyd:<type> <name>;
keyd:<type> <name> = <value>;

docs.title: Data Types
docs.text: Type Description Example tiro Integer 10, -5, 42 jajab Float 3.14, -2.5 eray String "Hello" xaraf Character 'A', 'B' miyaa Boolean haa (yes), maya (no) tix:<type> Array [1, 2, 3] qaamuus Dictionary {"key": "value"} waxbo Void/null No value

docs.title: Integer (tiro)
docs.text:
keyd:tiro a = 10;
keyd:tiro b;
keyd:tiro c = 5 + 3;

docs.title: Float (jajab)
docs.text:
keyd:jajab pi = 3.14;
keyd:jajab x = 2.5 * 4.2;

docs.title: String (eray)
docs.text:
keyd:eray name = "Hello";
keyd:eray greeting = name + " World";

docs.title: Character (xaraf)
docs.text:
keyd:xaraf ch = 'A';
keyd:xaraf ch2 = 'B';

docs.title: Boolean (miyaa)
docs.text:
keyd:miyaa flag1 = haa;
keyd:miyaa flag2 = maya;
keyd:miyaa flag3 = a > 5;

docs.title: Array (tix)
docs.text:
keyd:tix:tiro nums = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
keyd:tix:eray words = ["aa", "bb", "cc"];
keyd:tix:tiro empty = [];

docs.title: Dictionary (qaamuus)
docs.text:
keyd:qaamuus person = {"magac": "Ali", "da": 25};
keyd:qaamuus emptyDict;

docs.title: Void (waxbo)
docs.text: keyd:waxbo nothing;

docs.title: F-Strings
docs.text:
Use $"{}" for string interpolation:

keyd:eray name = "Tusmo";
keyd:tiro age = 5;
qor($"Name is {name}, Age is {age}");

docs.title: Ternary Operator
docs.text:
keyd:miyaa cond = a > 5;
keyd:eray result = cond ? "big" : "small";
